reviews.title: Not useful for Mortgages
reviews.body:
Whilst the app is alright - elements are large and clear, as a mortgage customer it doesn’t do much for me. It’s very good at showing me payments I have made which isn’t very helpful as they are pre-defined and don’t change. It’s also good at showing me the remaining balance of the mortgage but that’s not a fast moving milestone. It doesn’t show any indication of how much interest has been charged either each month or cumulative over the course of a year; a vital statistic for a borrowing product (the website is no better either). It also doesn’t allow overpayments. Overall it doesn’t really help with management of the product when it could do much more to help people manage their product. Information is essential for managing and understanding debt.

Edit: I wrote this review months ago. There have been no improvements in these key areas. I still have to write a letter to find out how much interest I’m paying and I still can’t make overpayments even though this was “coming soon”.

reviews.title: Registration and annoying withdrawal problems

The app booted me out of my account today because I reached the device limit. I logged in to the Skipton website and eventually found the required page. It turned out I only had 2 registered devices so it made no sense I had to delete one of them to use the app. The app should allow users to have more than one registered device without having to jump through hoops.

In addition to this you have to add a nominated account to withdraw funds to. That should be really easy but it’s far from it. I can see loads of other reviews have said this and I wish I saw them before opening an account with Skipton. It look over an hour to eventually add my account due to Skipton logging me out every time I verified who I was using the app. This process is incredibly frustrating because this should be a really easy to do and it’s as if they have made it intentionally obstructive to make withdrawals.

reviews.title: Skipton App
reviews.body:
I found App very confusing for the following reasons:
1) it is very difficult to log in, with endless messages like ‘it looks like you didn’t complete your registration, please register online’?!? I have done it many times! After ‘new’ registration, app session would usually report back that I registered too many devices, while I have only one!?! Very, very frustrating and not user friendly at all.
2) Once in the App ( or more often in website), layout is another confusion. People often want to see the state of their account and / or latest transactions. Hmm… another challenge. Eventually you can find it, but is far from obvious or intuitive. 
In essence, both web and app look nice with some lovely graphics, but the most important part, functionality and ease of access, are both left pretty inferior.

Developers if you’re reading this, PLEASE UPDATE THE APP WITH THIS BASIC FUNCTIONALITY :) :) :)

reviews.body: How exasperating! I just wanted to set up my online account so I can manage my mortgage, and it’s taken days to get the data aligned, and then not satisfied with my proving I am me, they had to send out a letter with a code, and by the time it arrived I had forgotten my login details and my computer hasn’t saved any of it. Whilst I appreciate the need to ensure my account is protected, I really don’t appreciate the aggravation completing the process. Now that I’ve remembered what I had set my login to, I got the app set up, and everything works, but when I say everything, that isn’t very much. It seems that despite the years of poor reviews saying the same thing, the app is little more than a bloated and unnecessary 2FA system. Now the ‘developers’ (for want of a polite word) can respond with their copy and paste excuses for not having a clue. Very very disappointed, thank you Skipton, you’ve evidently worked very hard to deliver this 2FA system, and clearly pay absolutely no attention to users feedback. As a pro developer myself, I would be embarrassed by the feedback, as would my employers.

There is nothing to show a “pending” transaction. I have had to go into the branch today, just to see whether the transaction I actioned on Thursday (two days ago) is being dealt with. I was assured that there is no sign of any transaction so I have today repeated it. Money is due to leave my account on Monday. If this is a duplicate of my original, my account will be emptied. 
I really don’t understand why a BACs transfer is not instant.
